prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel employee called a skiv has a property: it can be activated by one of its color-coded specialtys. Your job is to figure out which employee in the perception scenario is the skiv-analog, and therefore which color-coded specialty on it can be activated.

Memory scenario: There are three employees in an organization: a skiv, a tunk, and a drog. The skiv reports-to the tunk. The tunk sits-beside the drog. The skiv has a yellow color-coded specialty on top, a purple color-coded specialty on side, and a green color-coded specialty on bottom. The tunk has a purple color-coded specialty on top and a yellow color-coded specialty on bottom. The drog has a green color-coded specialty on top and a yellow color-coded specialty on side. Assigning the yellow specialty to the skiv activates it.

Perception scenario: There are three employees in an organization: a snig, a clop, and a trob. The trob reports-to the snig. The snig sits-beside the clop. The snig has a purple color-coded specialty on top and a yellow color-coded specialty on side. The clop has a green color-coded specialty on top and a purple color-coded specialty on bottom. The trob has a yellow color-coded specialty on top, a purple color-coded specialty on side, and a green color-coded specialty on bottom.

Which employee in the perception scenario is the skiv-analog, and which color-coded specialty activates it?
